props: variant?: default | destructive | outline | secondary | ghost | link | gradient | gradient-blue | gradient-cyan | gradient-green | blue | cyan | selected
size?: default | sm | lg | xl | icon | icon-sm | icon-lg
text?: bold | semibold | medium | normal | light

output: Renders the main shared button primitive or forwards styling onto a slotted child when asChild=true. It does not manage internal state, but it standardizes visual variants, hover motion, focus rings, disabled state, and event forwarding for actions across the app.
description: Most reused action component in the codebase (about 90 imports). It is the base CTA/control surface for forms, dialogs, tables, assignment flows, and shared state panels, and it centralizes the project's button styling system through class-variance-authority plus the shared hover wrapper.

props: Dialog accepts Radix Dialog root props such as open, defaultOpen, and onOpenChange
DialogContent accepts all Radix content props plus showCloseButton?: boolean and showOverlay?: boolean
Header/footer/title/description wrappers accept normal div/title/description props

output: Provides a complete modal primitive set with portal mounting, overlay rendering, close control, and animated content shell. Open/close state is controlled by the parent through Radix props, so side effects happen in consumers rather than inside this module.
description: Shared modal infrastructure used throughout forms, editor flows, and file/assignment management (about 38 imports). It wraps Radix dialog primitives with the project's overlay, spacing, animation, and close-button defaults so feature components can compose dialogs consistently.

output: Renders a ref-forwarding text/file/input field with consistent focus, invalid, disabled, and file-input styling. It has no internal state and simply forwards value/change handling to consumers.
description: Shared low-level form field used heavily across auth, CRM, grade editing, and content-management screens (about 33 imports). It normalizes baseline input visuals so business forms do not duplicate Tailwind classes.

props: Select accepts Radix Select root props such as value, defaultValue, onValueChange, open, and onOpenChange
SelectTrigger adds size?: sm | default and className?: string
SelectContent accepts Radix content props plus position?: item-aligned | popper and align?: string
SelectItem/Label/Separator accept the matching Radix primitive props

output: Returns a fully composed dropdown/select system with trigger, portal content, scrolling controls, labels, separators, and selectable items. Selection state is owned by the parent via Radix callbacks; this module mainly supplies behavior wrappers and styling.
description: Shared selection control used across attendance, auth, library, and scheduling flows (about 16 imports). It gives the app a consistent dropdown experience with blue chevrons, animated portal content, and reusable item rendering.

props: Popover accepts Radix Popover root props such as open, defaultOpen, and onOpenChange
PopoverContent accepts Radix content props plus align?: string, sideOffset?: number, and className?: string
Trigger/Anchor accept matching Radix primitive props

output: Creates anchored floating content via a portal with the project's default sizing, border, shadow, and entry/exit motion. Open state and any business logic remain external to the component consumers.
description: Reusable floating-surface primitive used by the phone input, sidebar controls, attendance tabs, and other contextual UI (about 13 imports). It standardizes how lightweight overlays are positioned and styled without forcing feature-specific behavior.

output: Renders a styled semantic label that respects disabled group/peer states. It has no local state and simply improves accessible form labeling.
description: Shared form-label primitive used throughout auth, settings, modals, and feedback flows (about 12 imports). It keeps text sizing, spacing, and disabled behavior consistent across the application's forms.

props: All Radix Checkbox root props such as checked, defaultChecked, and onCheckedChange

output: Renders a styled checkbox with an icon indicator and forwards controlled/uncontrolled checked state through Radix. It does not mutate data directly; consumers respond to onCheckedChange.
description: Shared boolean-input primitive used in dashboards, filters, attendance flows, and question selection UI (about 11 imports). It centralizes the project's checkbox visuals and checked-state treatment.

props: DropdownMenu accepts Radix root props such as open and onOpenChange
DropdownMenuContent adds sideOffset?: number and className?: string
DropdownMenuItem adds inset?: boolean and variant?: default | destructive
Checkbox/radio/sub-menu exports accept the corresponding Radix primitive props

output: Provides a complete contextual action menu system with nested content, checkbox/radio items, and portal-based positioning. State and action side effects are delegated to parent handlers attached to menu items.
description: Reusable context-menu/action-menu module used in comment cards, assignments, grade detail views, and journal controls (about 7 imports). It gives complex action menus a consistent interaction model and visual language.

props: TooltipProvider accepts delayDuration?: number plus Radix provider props
Tooltip accepts Radix tooltip root props
TooltipContent accepts Radix content props plus sideOffset?: number and className?: string

output: Creates lightweight hover/focus help content with a zero-delay provider by default and portal-mounted tooltip content. It does not own business state beyond transient tooltip visibility.
description: Shared micro-help primitive used in editor tools, whiteboard UI, student account switching, and classroom views (about 6 imports). It standardizes transient explanatory UI without each feature reimplementing provider setup.

props: All exports accept standard div props

output: Renders a composable card layout system for bordered content blocks with dedicated header/content/footer slots. There is no internal state; the output is purely structural and presentational.
description: Shared container module used by dashboards, feed cards, language chooser UI, and chart panels (about 5 imports). It gives feature teams a consistent card anatomy instead of copy-pasting shell markup.

output: Renders a styled multiline input with shared invalid/focus/disabled states. It does not keep local text state and relies on the parent for value and onChange.
description: Shared multiline text field used in modals, bug reporting, elective setup, and library forms (about 5 imports). It standardizes the app's editable text-area styling and sizing behavior.

output: Renders a full-screen blocking loading overlay with the Alims logo, inline keyframe styles, and a custom spinner/glow effect. It has no inputs and is used as a temporary UI state while auth/bootstrap requests are in progress.
description: Global full-screen loader used during app hydration and auth flows (about 5 imports). Unlike the smaller loading-state helpers, this component takes over the viewport and communicates application-level waiting states.

props: ErrorState/EmptyState/NotFoundState/UnauthorizedState: title?, description?, details?, className?, panelClassName?, iconClassName?, titleClassName?, descriptionClassName?, detailsClassName?
Optional action object: { label: string, onClick: () => void, disabled?: boolean, variant?: Button variant }

output: Renders standardized status panels and loaders for loading, error, empty, not-found, and unauthorized conditions. The only side effect is invoking an optional action callback through the shared Button; all data fetching and retry logic lives in consumers.
description: Cross-feature state-feedback module used in classroom lists, vice-principal student pages, and subject pages (about 4 imports). It consolidates repeated empty/error/loading UI into a consistent panel system with optional recovery actions.

output: Renders a composed phone field with a country selector popover, search box, dial-code picker, and formatted local number input. Internally it manages popover open state, search query, selected country, and local number, then emits a normalized full phone number string through onChange.
description: Shared enriched form control used in recovery, registration, and staff-management flows (about 3 imports). It encapsulates country metadata, parsing, formatting, and picker UX so screens receive a single canonical phone string instead of rebuilding the logic.

props: Each export accepts the native props for its HTML table element

output: Provides a composable table shell with a scroll container plus standardized row, cell, header, and caption styling. It does not manage sorting, selection, or pagination; that behavior stays in consuming features.
description: Shared tabular layout module used in capacity views, vice-principal schedule matching, absence dashboards, and time-selection UI (about 4 imports). It acts as the styling baseline for any feature that needs raw table markup.

output: Renders a pill-style tab button for switching between legacy/unmount screens and password/schedule subviews. It has no internal state and notifies the parent through onClick when the tab is selected.
description: Small but repeatedly reused tab control in the unmount area and password/schedule screens (about 5 imports). It standardizes the active/inactive visual treatment for those segmented button groups.

output: Renders a reusable left-arrow back button with optional custom label text. It does not handle navigation itself; consumers pass the desired back action through onClick.
description: Frequently reused navigation affordance in unmount flows and restrictions screens (about 7 imports). It keeps back-navigation controls visually consistent across otherwise separate admin forms.

output: Renders either a teacher-style inline trigger or a student-style floating/header trigger, opens a feedback dialog, manages description/rating/file state, listens for global paste and drag-drop while open, and submits data through GraphQL plus direct attachment uploads. On submit it runs the BUG_REPORT_CREATE mutation, requests upload URLs through the BUG_REPORT_ATTACHMENT_UPLOAD_URL query, uploads files with PUT requests, shows success/error states, and resets local state when the dialog closes or succeeds.
description: Shared cross-feature feedback component mounted in multiple high-visibility surfaces including the student shell, book parser header, subject topic layout, and legacy test header (about 4 imports). It is one of the few truly global interactive components because it couples UI, router context, GraphQL mutations, and file-upload behavior into a single reusable entry point for bug reporting.

output: Renders two shared student-shell layout wrappers: a full-height outer page container and a scrollable content region with standard student padding and scrollbar behavior. These components do not manage business state; they provide consistent layout boundaries for student-facing pages.
description: Shared student layout primitives reused by the student shell plus grades, leaderboard, and tasks pages (about 4 imports). They keep viewport locking, padding, and scrolling behavior consistent across the student experience.

output: Reads the current router pathname, derives role-aware breadcrumb items, translates known route segments, hides dynamic ID segments, optionally collapses long trails with an ellipsis, and renders a fixed breadcrumb bar. It does not mutate data; it reacts to router state and translation context.
description: App-shell navigation component mounted globally in the admin shell. Even though it is not imported in many files directly, it is effectively a global component because every admin page can render through it, and it centralizes route-to-breadcrumb logic instead of duplicating that work in page components.

props: principalButtons?: Array<{ id: string; label: string; onClick?: () => void }>
zavuchButtons?: Array<{ id: string; label: string; onClick?: () => void }>
classTeacherButtons?: Array<{ id: string; label: string; onClick?: () => void }>
teacherButtons?: Array<{ id: string; label: string; onClick?: () => void }>
Standard div HTML props via React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>

output: Renders the main admin sidebar with grouped role-specific menu sections, a collapsible icon-only state, guided/highlighted items, and click-outside closing behavior. It reads auth-store role data, lazily fetches the employee role after hydration when missing, tracks active/open group state locally, and forwards menu selection/open-close events back to parent shell handlers.
description: Primary global navigation surface for the admin shell. It is not a tiny reusable control, but it is an app-wide component because it orchestrates the navigation experience for principal, vice-principal, class-teacher, and teacher roles from one shared implementation.
